en.name: Mediterranean Innovation Alliance for sustainable blue economy
en.description: The BLUEBIOMED strategic project promotes the transformation of Mediterranean blue bioeconomy field toward sustainable development goals through innovation. The project supports better governance of innovation policies bridging the transnational governance frameworks active in the Mediterranean area (i.e. UfM, UNEP-MAP, EUSAIR, WestMed, Bluemed Initiative, etc.) with the territorial - regional/national – policy making, assuming the Agenda 2030 Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) as the common reference grid. R&I strategies for smart specialisation (RIS3) are the key entry point to understand and orient the innovation trends and priorities across MED regions and connect transnational cooperation with investments for growth and jobs of the Cohesion Policy.BLUEBIOMED will develop a governance model of innovation policies to tackle complex challenges and will test the innovative approach through policy experiments triggering the cooperation of the quadruple helix stakeholders for interregional innovation investments. The project will be an open platform for an inclusive co-design process with the wider innovation community promoting a bottom-up MED Innovation Alliance for blue bioeconomy to streamline multiple efforts and initiatives around the Mediterranean. BLUEBIOMED will support the start-up of the Alliance to boost the Mediterranean leadership in transformative innovation of the blue economy.
en.expected_results: 1 Better coherence of innovation policies for blue bioeconomy at different levels of governmen : Multiple institutions and actors at different levels of governance and with variable geography play a role in the blue bioeconomy innovation policy making process. Strategies and plans are not always interoperable and mutually connected and there is room to improve vertical, horizontal and temporal coherence. As a result of the project transnational and territorial levels of policies will be bridged in a coherent framework inspired by the Agenda 2030 SDGs, building a consensus on the most relevant MED challenges to be addressed and the priorities for joint transnational action . This result is the first step toward an evidence-based articulation of a MED innovation alliance for blue bioeconomy, bridging different policy frameworks, identifying common priorities along with gaps to be filled.1 Better coherence of innovation policies for blue bioeconomy at different levels of governmen : Multiple institutions and actors at different levels of governance and with variable geography play a role in the blue bioeconomy innovation policy making process. Strategies and plans are not always interoperable and mutually connected and there is room to improve vertical, horizontal and temporal coherence. As a result of the project transnational and territorial levels of policies will be bridged in a coherent framework inspired by the Agenda 2030 SDGs, building a consensus on the most relevant MED challenges to be addressed and the priorities for joint transnational action . This result is the first step toward an evidence-based articulation of a MED innovation alliance for blue bioeconomy, bridging different policy frameworks, identifying common priorities along with gaps to be filled.2 Improved capacity to orient innovation to address complex societal challenges : Besides improving the multilevel linkages among policy frameworks it is necessary to improve the capacity to orient innovation policy to address complex societal challenges. This implies making a step further from shared objectives and targets to the design of the whole roadmap to translate the objectives in concrete policy mechanisms and investments (projects).Improved capacity means better understanding of the contribution of innovation (technological, organisational, social) to tackle the challenges, including the time dimension of the impact. It also means a more open and inclusive way to design and implement policies and governance mechanisms, in the wake of RRI (responsible research and innovation) approaches.This result is therefore linked to the development of forefront policy means to articulate the MED innovation alliance for blue bioeconomy.3 Reinforced transnational MED Blue Bioeconomy Innovation Community : This result is twofold. It means a higher propensity of innovation actors from the quadruple helix (government, research/university, business, civil society) ecosystem to engage in transnational cooperation to mobilise the innovation potential toward the sustainable development goals.At a broader level, it means a flexible but more stable framework to foster this cooperation in the long run. The reinforcement of the innovation community comes streamlining - rather than duplicating - the efforts of already existing transnational and national strategies and initiatives, with a generative business model in the context of the next programming period.
en.achievements: In the third semester the project entered its maturity phase and all technical work packages have produced their deliverables. The studying activities, that were already in advanced stage of implementation during the previous semester, were finalized to complete two important deliverables: the RIS3 mapping report based on desk analysis and primary data collection with the attached country/region policy factsheets; the technology and market forecast report to highlight the most promising innovation areas linked to blue bioeconomy.The testing WP has been largely implemented during the period, with the organisation of the multi stakeholder workshops of the four policy experiments that ran in parallel under the coordination of CREDA, ART-ER, IFAPA and Demokritos. Hundreds of people from 94 organisations have been involved, overcoming the initial forecast in the AF.The capitalization activities to startup of the Mediterraean innovation alliance for sustainable blue bioeconomy (MedIA-sbb) progressed in parallel. The Call to action was approved in its definitive version and published, endorsed by the Regions of the CRPM Intermediterranean commission. A co-design process to develop and start-up the alliance was launched, in collaboration with B-BLUE and supported by the BG Community and PANORAAMEd. The first edition of the regional innovation forum, organized by ART-ER in Ferrara (18-19 November at Sealogy) has hosted also the first in person MedIA in person co-design workshop. The event registered a wide and qualified participation, including the speak of the representatives appointed in the Sounding Board who endorsed the launch of the Alliance. A MedIA scoping paper has been drafted and shared with potential members and progressively updated with the results of following contacts and co-design workshop. A second one was organized online in February and other will follow in the following period.Management and communication WPs progressed regularly. The partnership thoroughly assessed the state of advancement of activities and budget consumption. The project steering Group dediced to ask the extension of the duration until September 2022, without endorsing in a major budget revision.Communication accompanied the technical activities, produced ad-hoc materials (articles, news, press release, animated videos) about the Call to action, the policy experiment, the forum. Networking with other projects and stakeholders was finalized towards the MedIA.
en.expected_outputs: Stakeholders involved in policy experiments :50,Stakeholders involved in the MED Innovation Alliance :80
en.delivered_outputs: Number of stakeholders (structures) involved in supported initiatives (per category representing public and private stakeholders), Expenditures declared to the EC for Axis 4,

fr.description: Le projet stratégique BLUEBIOMED pousse la transformation par l’innovation du domaine de la bioéconomie bleue Méditerranéen vers des objectifs de développement durable. Le projet soutient une meilleure gouvernance des politiques d'innovation reliant les cadres de gouvernance transnationaux de la Méditerranée (ex:UpM ,UNEP-MAP,EUSAIR,WestMed,Bluemed,etc) avec l'élaboration des politiques territoriales régionales/nationales, considérant l'Agenda 2030 des Objectifs de développement durable une grille de référence commune. Les stratégies R&D pour la spécialisation intelligente RIS3 sont le point d'entrée clé pour comprendre et diriger les tendances et priorités en matière d'innovation dans les régions MED, relier la coopération transnationale aux investissements pour la croissance et l'emploi de la politique de cohésion. BLUBIOMED développera un modèle de gouvernance des politiques d'innovation pour relever des défis complexes et testera l'approche innovante par des expériences déclenchant la coopération des acteurs de la quadruple hélix pour les investissements innovants interrégionaux. Le projet sera une plate-forme ouverte pour un processus de co-conception avec la communauté de l'innovation, promouvant une Alliance pour l’Innovation MED pour la bioéconomie bleue pour rationaliser les multiples efforts et initiatives dans la Méditerranée. BLUEBIOMED soutiendra le démarrage de l'Alliance pour dynamiser le leadership méditerranéen vers une innovation profonde de l'économie bleue
fr.delivered_outputs: Nombre de stakeholders (structures) impliquées dans initiatives soutenues (par représentation de catégorie d'organisations publiques et privées), Dépenses déclarées à la CE pour Axe 4,

en.name: Building the blue biotechnology community in the Mediterranean
en.description: 10 partners with proved experience in the Blue Bioeconomy field from 8 Med countries and more than 300 Med stakeholders from universities, research centers, public authorities, business support organizations & Med multilateral organizations, working together for 22 months to create the Blue Biotechnologies (BBt) community in the Mediterranean. The exploitation of marine bio-resources through biotechnological solutions is a field with massive potential for innovation & economic growth, but it is a relatively young discipline, so opportunities & key enabling factors need a coordination.B-Blue project aims at gathering the key actors of the Med BBt sector and increase their innovation capacity and their coordination in order to unlock the innovation potential in the field through joint transnational initiatives, involving also organizations from the Southern Shore of the Mediterranean. The transnational coordination framework, the project aims to create, is based on an inclusive quintuple-helix approach always including the socio-environmental perspective in the decisional process and building on a common knowledge ground selected on the basis of its potential of addressing the SGDs at Med level. The B-Blue work towards to the implementation of a transnational coordination mechanism for the BBt community through the mutual interconnection the digital BBt community platform & a Med network of territorial based-collaborative space on selected BBt value chains (BBt HUBs).
en.expected_results: 1 Action plan for implementation at territorial level of a joint transnational cooperative framework to develop coordinated actions on BBt value chains : The project carries out an action plan for the Med Network of BBt HUBs activation, growth & connection with wider networks having the same territorial, collaborative & inclusive quintuple-helix approach the BBt HUBs are based on. The action plan aims at i) strengthening the connection among the BBt HUBs activated through the implementation of pilot actions by PPs (WP4) in 5 different territories of project area; ii) defining strategic actions to guarantee the outliving of the BBt project network after the its end; iii) enlarging the BBt network through the replication of the BBT HUBs framework implementation in other territories (including the MENA countries); iv) enlarging the activities of each HUB replicating the approach & the vertical actions developed on specific value chains in other values chains of the BBt sector; v) connecting the project network to already existing & wider networks with the same inclusive & multi-actors approach (e.g. ENOLL: EU Network of Living Labs). This specific project result contributes to the program result indicator through a joint strategic document allowing the implementation of a coordinated operation at Med level aiming at the establishment & growth of a Med network of HUBs. It can support the implementation of future joint transnational initiative on specific value chains &/or define common need & challenges to develop joint programs & coordinated local initiatives (e.g. coordination among regional funds in the BBt sector).1 Action plan for implementation at territorial level of a joint transnational cooperative framework to develop coordinated actions on BBt value chains : The project carries out an action plan for the Med Network of BBt HUBs activation, growth & connection with wider networks having the same territorial, collaborative & inclusive quintuple-helix approach the BBt HUBs are based on. The action plan aims at i) strengthening the connection among the BBt HUBs activated through the implementation of pilot actions by PPs (WP4) in 5 different territories of project area; ii) defining strategic actions to guarantee the outliving of the BBt project network after the its end; iii) enlarging the BBt network through the replication of the BBT HUBs framework implementation in other territories (including the MENA countries); iv) enlarging the activities of each HUB replicating the approach & the vertical actions developed on specific value chains in other values chains of the BBt sector; v) connecting the project network to already existing & wider networks with the same inclusive & multi-actors approach (e.g. ENOLL: EU Network of Living Labs). This specific project result contributes to the program result indicator through a joint strategic document allowing the implementation of a coordinated operation at Med level aiming at the establishment & growth of a Med network of HUBs. It can support the implementation of future joint transnational initiative on specific value chains &/or define common need & challenges to develop joint programs & coordinated local initiatives (e.g. coordination among regional funds in the BBt sector).2 1 Policy paper with recommendations to improve coordination among EU Cohesion Programs & Med Policy Schemes for Innovation in the BBt sector : The project releases a recommendations document for Macro Regional Strategies for the Cohesion Policy programming period 2021-2027 (Mainstream & Territorial Cooperation) to be shared with policy makers, agencies, clusters, industrial associations & program owners, as well as with the CICERONE project’s platform. The document is based on: i) the results of the testing phase both on transnational vertical actions on specific value chains & on the inclusive soft governance approach representing the common basis for the BBt HUBs development; ii) the outcomes of the transnational events on innovation transfer & matchmaking activities of the WP5; iii) the identified connection between BBt sector & both SDGs & RIS3 & iv) priorities & actions set by the CICERONE SRIA for the Blue Economy sector (Territory & Sea section of the document). The document is aimed at supporting the process of strengthening multilateral coordination among the regional cohesion funds & policies for innovations in the BBt sector so that Med joint responses could be developed to answer identified common challenges. The document (result of leveraging mechanism) while contributing to the achievement of this objective, is also contributing to the program result indicator since it supports the implementation of coordinated strategic operation at Med level on the BBt sector.3 1 Strategic action plan for BBt innovation community Cooperation mechanism formalization in view of the Med Alliance : The project carries out an action plan for the (formal/informal) establishment a BBt community for innovation which could follow 1 of the cooperation mechanism already developed to foster innovation in other sectors/topics (EIT KIC, Horizon partnerships, other frameworks/agreements). This project result supports the implementation of a joint framework for the future strategic cooperation in the BBt sector at Med level through definition of actions for cooperation mechanism within its innovation community. The BBt community is built through: i) capitalization of existing knowledge in desk studies (definition of BBt innovation frontrunner, WP3); ii) the territorial actions in the HUBs (Sherpa Group, WP4) & iii) the transnational innovation transfer events (WP5) & the digital platform gathering the Med BBt organizations The action plan is aimed at its systematization, formalization & mid-long term sustainability. Moreover, the BBt innovation community will be part of the Blue Bio Med Alliance on the Blue Bioeconomy developed by BlueBioMed project. B-Blue project is a contributor of the Alliance thanks to the modular & coordinated activities foreseen between the 2 projects & its PPs will be among the founders. The action plan & the proposed BBt innovation community’s cooperation mechanism in itself are both multilateral frameworks which allow coordinated operations to stimulate future Med joint multilateral actions to respond to identified common challenges, thus contributing to the program indicator result.
en.achievements: The project is proceeding beyond the expectation: it is getting a strong mediatic attention and many organizations participate to the project activities to the project activities and/or joined the events organized by the project. In fact aroud 400 stakeholders have been involved so far and many more have been reached through the project communication channels. More than 5.000 of them directly interacted with the project communication channels and we estimated that at least 10 times more are the persons already informed about the project thanks to information shared thorugh different channel and to the project partners participation to external events.
en.expected_outputs: Territorial BBt HUBs establishment :120,B-Blue digital platform activation & innovation knowledge transfer package :100,BBt innovation community cooperation mechanism :10,Coordinated BBt ecosystem supporting alignment of innovation policies :20,BBt HUBs network :50
en.delivered_outputs: Number of stakeholders (structures) involved in supported initiatives (per category representing public and private stakeholders), Expenditures declared to the EC for Axis 4,

fr.name: Construire la communauté des biotechnologies bleues en la Méditerranée
fr.description: D’une durée de 22 mois, le projet B-Blue réunit 10 partenaires, ayant une forte expérience dans le domaine de la bioéconomie bleue et vise à créer une Communauté Bleue des acteurs de la Biotechnologie (BBt) en Méditerranée. L'exploitation des bio-ressources marines par le biais de solutions biotechnologiques est un domaine à fort potentiel de croissance et d'innovation. Il s'agit toutefois d'une discipline relativement jeune, qui doit encore se structurer. Le projet B-Blue vise à rassembler plus de 300 parties prenantes positionnées sur différentes chaines de valeur des biotechnologies afin d’accroitre le potentiel d'innovation sur le terrain via des initiatives collaboratives et transnationales, impliquant également des organisations de la rive sud de la Méditerranée. Le cadre de coopération transnationale, que le projet vise à établir, est basé sur une approche inclusive de la quintuple hélice incluant les questions socio-environnementales dans le processus décisionnel et s'appuyant sur un terrain de connaissances communes sélectionné sur son potentiel à aborder les ODD. B-Blue favorise la synergie entre les acteurs méditerranéens ainsi que la consolidation et le partage de connaissances au travers la création de hubs régionaux, regroupant sur chaque territoire des acteurs de chaines de valeurs sélectionnées (BBt HUBs) et la création d’une plate-forme numérique regroupant les acteurs de la communauté au niveau méditerranéen.
fr.expected_results: 1 1 plan d'action pour la mise en œuvre au niveau territorial d'un cadre coopératif transnational conjoint pour développer : Le projet met en œuvre un plan d'action pour l'activation, la croissance et la connexion du réseau Med de BBt HUBs avec des réseaux plus larges ayant la même approche territoriale, collaborative et inclusive d'hélice quintuple sur laquelle les BBt HUBs sont basés.Le plan d'action vise à i) renforcer la connexion entre les HUB BBt activés par la mise en œuvre d'actions pilotes par des PP (WP4) dans 5 territoires différents de la zone du projet; ii) définir des actions stratégiques pour garantir la pérennité du réseau du projet BBt après sa fin; iii) l'élargissement du réseau BBt par la réplication de la mise en œuvre du cadre BBT HUBs dans d'autres territoires (y compris les pays MENA); iv) élargir les activités de chaque HUB en reproduisant l'approche et les actions verticales développées sur des chaînes de valeur spécifiques dans d'autres chaînes de valeur du secteur BBt; v) connecter le réseau du projet à des réseaux déjà existants et plus larges avec la même approche inclusive et multi-acteurs (par exemple ENOLL: EU Network of Living Labs).Ce résultat spécifique du projet contribue à l'indicateur de résultat du programme à travers un document stratégique commun permettant la mise en œuvre d'une opération coordonnée au niveau Med visant à la mise en place et à la croissance d'un réseau Med de HUB. Il peut soutenir la mise en œuvre de la future initiative transnationale conjointe sur des chaînes de valeur spécifiques et / ou définir des besoins et des défis communs pour développer des programmes conjoints et des initiatives locales coordonnées (par exemple, la coordination entre les fonds régionaux dans le secteur BBt).1 1 plan d'action pour la mise en œuvre au niveau territorial d'un cadre coopératif transnational conjoint pour développer : Le projet met en œuvre un plan d'action pour l'activation, la croissance et la connexion du réseau Med de BBt HUBs avec des réseaux plus larges ayant la même approche territoriale, collaborative et inclusive d'hélice quintuple sur laquelle les BBt HUBs sont basés.Le plan d'action vise à i) renforcer la connexion entre les HUB BBt activés par la mise en œuvre d'actions pilotes par des PP (WP4) dans 5 territoires différents de la zone du projet; ii) définir des actions stratégiques pour garantir la pérennité du réseau du projet BBt après sa fin; iii) l'élargissement du réseau BBt par la réplication de la mise en œuvre du cadre BBT HUBs dans d'autres territoires (y compris les pays MENA); iv) élargir les activités de chaque HUB en reproduisant l'approche et les actions verticales développées sur des chaînes de valeur spécifiques dans d'autres chaînes de valeur du secteur BBt; v) connecter le réseau du projet à des réseaux déjà existants et plus larges avec la même approche inclusive et multi-acteurs (par exemple ENOLL: EU Network of Living Labs).Ce résultat spécifique du projet contribue à l'indicateur de résultat du programme à travers un document stratégique commun permettant la mise en œuvre d'une opération coordonnée au niveau Med visant à la mise en place et à la croissance d'un réseau Med de HUB. Il peut soutenir la mise en œuvre de la future initiative transnationale conjointe sur des chaînes de valeur spécifiques et / ou définir des besoins et des défis communs pour développer des programmes conjoints et des initiatives locales coordonnées (par exemple, la coordination entre les fonds régionaux dans le secteur BBt).2 Document politique contenant des recommandations pour améliorer la coordination entre les programmes de cohésion de l'UE & les : Le projet publie un document de recommandations pour les Stratégies Macro Régionales pour la période de programmation de la Politique de Cohésion 2021-2027 (Mainstream & Coopération Territoriale) à partager avec les décideurs politiques, les agences, les clusters, les associations industrielles et les propriétaires de programmes, ainsi qu'avec la plateforme du projet CICERONE .Le document est basé sur:i) les résultats de la phase de test à la fois sur les actions verticales transnationales sur des chaînes de valeur spécifiques et sur l'approche de gouvernance douce inclusive représentant la base commune pour le développement des HUB BBt; ii) les résultats des événements transnationaux sur le transfert d'innovation et les activités de jumelage du WP5; iii) le lien identifié entre le secteur BBt & les ODD & RIS3 & iv) les priorités & les actions fixées par le CICERONE SRIA pour le secteur de l'Economie Bleue.Le document vise à soutenir le processus de renforcement de la coordination multilatérale entre le fonds de cohésion régional et les politiques pour les innovations dans le secteur BBt afin que des réponses conjointes Med puissent être développées pour répondre aux défis communs identifiés. Le document contribuant à la réalisation de cet objectif contribue également à l'indicateur de résultat du programme car il soutient la mise en œuvre d'une opération stratégique coordonnée au niveau Med sur le secteur BBt.3 1 Plan d'action stratégique pour la communauté de l'innovation BBt Formalisation du mécanisme de coopération en : Le projet met en œuvre un plan d'action pour la création (formelle / informelle) d'une communauté BBt pour l'innovation qui pourrait suivre l'un des mécanismes de coopération déjà développés pour favoriser l'innovation dans d'autres secteurs / thèmes (EIT KIC, partenariats Horizon, autres cadres / accords) .Le résultat de ce projet soutient la mise en œuvre d'un cadre commun pour la future coopération stratégique dans le secteur BBt au niveau Med à travers la définition d'actions de mécanisme de coopération au sein de sa communauté de l'innovation.La communauté BBt se construit à travers:i) capitalisation des connaissances existantes dans les études documentaires (définition de BBt innovation frontrunner, WP3), les actions territoriales dans les HUB (Sherpa Group, WP4) & la plateforme numérique (+ outil de matchmaking) & les événements transnationaux de transfert d'innovation (WP5);& le plan d'action vise sa systématisation, sa formalisation et sa durabilité à moyen et long terme. De plus, la communauté de l'innovation BBt fait partie de la Blue Bio Med Alliance sur la Blue Bioeconomy développée par le projet BlueBioMed. Le projet B-Blue est un contributeur de l'Alliance grâce aux activités modulaires et coordonnées prévues entre les 2 projets et ses PP seront parmi les fondateurs. Le plan d'action et le mécanisme de coopération proposé par la communauté de l'innovation BBt sont en eux-mêmes des cadres multilatéraux qui permettent des opérations coordonnéesstimuler les futures actions multilatérales conjointes Med pour répondre aux défis communs identifiés, en contribuant au résultat de l'indicateur de programme.
fr.expected_outputs: Mise en place des territoriaux HUbs des BBt sector :120,Activation de la plateforme numérique du project B-Blue & Transfert de trousse de connaissances sur l'innovation du projet B-Blue :100,Mécanisme de coopération communautaire de l'innovation BBt :10,Écosystème des BBt coordonné l'alignement des politiques d'innovation :20,Network des HUBs des BBt :50
fr.delivered_outputs: Nombre de stakeholders (structures) impliquées dans initiatives soutenues (par représentation de catégorie d'organisations publiques et privées), Dépenses déclarées à la CE pour Axe 4,

en.name: Winter Islands Network for all year round Tourism ExpeRience in the MEDiterranean
en.description: WINTER MED promotes the transition from the current overexploitation of insular tourist destinations during the summer to an alternative, sustainable, all-year round use of these territories, by delivering a transnational strategy and the tools for changing and upgrading one of the key sectors of the Mediterranean area. The project is built on the potential of capitalisation: analysing, assembling, adapting and testing available tools and methodologies to deliver a transnational strategy for the development of all-year round sustainable and responsible tourism in Mediterranean island destinations.The partners co-construct integrated solutions to common challenges, focusing in the common interests, beyond and besides the dimensions usually associated to the competition between destinations in a global market. From this objective and approach stems the holistic intervention aligned and contributing to ICZM and MSP in the area.The final transnational strategy provides the tools for and the demonstration of the benefits of policy learning and evidence-based decision-making. The partnership gathers a group of institutions with recognised experience in EU projects and tourism in insular areas. Furthermore, their complementary capacities allow the deployment of the sequential work plan: from the analysis of previous results to its implementation and improvement towards mainstreaming the project conclusions and widening their integration into the different policy and economic levels
en.expected_results: 1 Improvement of the MED transnational strategies for the reduction of the seasonality of tourist demand in island destinations : This result will focus on the exploitation of the main lessons learnt by past and ongoing projects focusing on sustainable M&C tourism that will be merged in a first draft of a joint transnational strategy for Mediterranean islands as a unique all year round destination. The outputs and results of successful MED (Mitomed+, Medcycletour, AlterEco), Interreg Europe (Brandtour), Italy - France Marittime (Smart Destination, Intense), H2020 (Civitas Destinations) projects will thus be integrated into a draft of the WM strategy for off-season tourism development. A self-assessment toolbox on which basis to identify the most appropriate strategies for each tourism destination will be developed.This result joins NECSTouR vision and approach to work for the tourism of tomorrow and it contributes the NECSTouR strategy 2019-2021 which sets out five key priorities: smart destinations, socio-cultural balance, skills and talent, safety and resilience and statistics and measurability; dealing with the seasonality and unbalanced tourism demand in the Mediterranean M&C regions, which will pave the way to the transfer and capitalization of the project results within and beyond the participating regions.This result contributes to the output indicators 3.1.a. No. of instruments available to enhance the development of sustainable and responsible tourism and 3.1.b Number of tourist destinations covered by a sustainable tourism evaluation tool1 Improvement of the MED transnational strategies for the reduction of the seasonality of tourist demand in island destinations : This result will focus on the exploitation of the main lessons learnt by past and ongoing projects focusing on sustainable M&C tourism that will be merged in a first draft of a joint transnational strategy for Mediterranean islands as a unique all year round destination. The outputs and results of successful MED (Mitomed+, Medcycletour, AlterEco), Interreg Europe (Brandtour), Italy - France Marittime (Smart Destination, Intense), H2020 (Civitas Destinations) projects will thus be integrated into a draft of the WM strategy for off-season tourism development. A self-assessment toolbox on which basis to identify the most appropriate strategies for each tourism destination will be developed.This result joins NECSTouR vision and approach to work for the tourism of tomorrow and it contributes the NECSTouR strategy 2019-2021 which sets out five key priorities: smart destinations, socio-cultural balance, skills and talent, safety and resilience and statistics and measurability; dealing with the seasonality and unbalanced tourism demand in the Mediterranean M&C regions, which will pave the way to the transfer and capitalization of the project results within and beyond the participating regions.This result contributes to the output indicators 3.1.a. No. of instruments available to enhance the development of sustainable and responsible tourism and 3.1.b Number of tourist destinations covered by a sustainable tourism evaluation tool2 Improvement of the regional / national action plans and policies for a more sustainable and responsible M&C tourism in the MED islands : This result builds upon the lessons learnt from the previous phase and provide for their integration into relevant regional / national action plans and policies of the partners’ regions by taking into due account the peculiarities of each destination. The regional action plans will employ an ecosystem-based approach in line with MPS and ICZM principles, ensuring their integration in regional/national programming documents. The mainstreaming of the project results into local, regional and national policies will be supported by capacity building and policy learning programmes to integrate the top-down approach of the previous phase with a bottom-up approach allowing local stakeholders to co-create policies and measures towards the extension of the tourism season in a sustainable manner. These activities will be developed and implemented by each partner to provide in depth training and to enhance skills needed for planning, development and implementation of relevant policies and strategies. The validation of the results coming out from these activities will lead to the consolidation of the final transnational strategy for all year round tourism in the MED islands.This result contributes to the output indicator 3.1.c. Number of strategies applying sustainable tourism management criteria.3 Scaling out of the the WINTER MED transnational strategy beyond participating regions through an EU wide advocacy campaign and dissemination action : This result will contribute to scale out the WM transnational strategy beyond the partners' regions. For this purpose, an EU wide advocacy campaign will be carried out to assure the policy uptake and scaling out of the WM Strategy and its adoption by relevant regional and national public authorities and other stakeholders in the MED area. Thanks to the contribution of CPMR, NECSTouR, partners the strategy will be disseminated across a significant number of MED Regions and beyond and call on regional governments to exploit its results to promote and implement policies for the transition from seasonal to a sustainable M&C all year round tourism.Policy recommendations will support this process so to influence policy decisions at the highest levels of decision-making by integrating the lessons learned during the project and by providing evidence on measures and policies successfully adopted in partner regions. This result contributes to the output indicator 3.1.a. Number of instruments available to enhance the development of sustainable and responsible tourism and 3.1.d. Number of regions and sub-regions engaged in implementing sustainable tourism plans.
en.achievements:
The last semester of WINTERMED project was dedicated to capitalisation and dissemination activities with the aim to mainstream the project results beyond participating territories. Over the past two and a half years the partnership has been working towards sustainable development in the Mediterranean region, with a particular focus on islands, transforming traditional tourism into a year-round sustainable economy. This project, carried out in a participatory way, developed tools to enable tourism stakeholders to become more competent in terms of sustainable tourism planning, as well as a transnational year-round tourism strategy. The partners translated the main lessons learned during this process in WINTERMED Policy paper, that was published at the beginning of the year. The document advocates for a common commitment of tourism stakeholders and different governance levels to a "Year-round Tourism Strategy for the Mediterranean Islands". A Memorandum of Understanding was delivered as well following the final approval of the policy paper. These two documents were the basis of capitalisation activities that took place in the last semester. The EU wide advocacy campaign was carried out to promote WINTER MED's joint vision for sustainable and responsible year-round tourism in Mediterranean island destinations. Under the title #YearRoundMed, the campaign aimed to engage key sustainable tourism stakeholders. The WINTER MED partnership organised its 2nd Engagement Forum back-to-back the CPMR–Intermediterranean Commission Task Force on Culture and Sustainable Tourism, held online on 25th of May, 

2022. The aim was to present project final results and to invite the participating organisations to endorse project outputs by signing the MoU. Project partners organised several capitalisation / dissemination workshops with the same objective. On 31st May the WINTER MED project organised its Final Project and Capitalisation Event "Shared vision and commitment to year-round sustainable tourism in Mediterranean island destinations and beyond" in Rhodes (Greece) and online. This high level policy event was the occasion to share the key results of 3 years of strong cooperation for more responsible and sustainable tourism in Mediterranean Island destinations. As a result of the capitalisation activities, the WM Strategy, the Policy recommendations and the MoU were endorsed by a wide range of administrations and organisation. 37 are the final signatories of the MoU.
en.expected_outputs: WINTER MED Self assessment toolbox :6, Regional Action Plans :6, WINTER MED transnational Strategy for all year round tourism in Mediterranean Island Destinations :1, Protocol agreements with the destinations joining the WINTER MED partnership :10, Policy recommendations :1.
en.delivered_outputs: Number of instruments available to enhance the development of sustainable and responsible tourism, Number of tourist destinations covered by a sustainable tourism evaluation tool, Number of strategies applying sustainable tourism management criteria, Number of regions and sub-regions engaged (through charters, protocols, MoU) in implementing sustainable tourism plans, Expenditures declared to the EC for Axis 3,

fr.description: WinterMed vise la transition de la surexploitation actuelle des destinations touristiques insulaires pendant l'été vers une utilisation alternative, durable et permanente de ces territoires, en proposant une stratégie transnationale et des outils pour changer et moderniser ce secteur-clé de la Méditerranée. Le projet est axé sur la capitalisation: analyser, assembler, adapter et tester les outils et méthodologies disponibles pour livrer une stratégie transnationale pour le développement d'un tourisme durable et responsable tout au long de l'année dans les destinations insulaires méditerranéennes. Les partenaires co-construisent des solutions intégrées à des défis communs, en se focalisant sur les intérêts communs, au-delà des aspects liés à la concurrence entre territoires dans le marché global. Cette approche induit l'intervention holistique alignée avec et contribuant à la GIZC et à la PSM dans la région. La stratégie transnationale finale offre les outils et la preuve des atouts de l'apprentissage politique et de la prise de décision basée sur des données factuelles. Le partenariat rassemble des institutions aux parcours reconnus sur les projets européens comme sur le tourisme en zones insulaires. La complémentarité de leurs capacités assure la réussite du plan de travail séquentiel: de l'analyse des résultats disponibles au teste et son amélioration vers l'intégration des conclusions du projet et l'élargissement du transfert aux différents niveaux politiques etéconomiques
fr.delivered_outputs: Nombre d'instruments disponibles pour améliorer le développement de tourisme durable et responsable, Nombre de destinations touristiques couvertes par un outil d'évaluation de tourisme durable, Nombre de stratégies appliquant des critères de gestion de tourisme durables, Nombre de régions et des sous-régions engagées (par des chartes, des protocoles, le MoU) dans la mise en œuvre de plans de tourisme durables, Dépenses déclarées à la CE pour Axe 3,

en.name: Governance and management of Posidonia beach- dune systems across the Mediterranean
en.description: The Posbemed 2 project will gather for the first time the key stakeholders involved in coastal management across the Mediterranean to work together in testing actions for a sustainable management of Posidonia landscape and beaches with Posidonia banquettes in Protected Areas(PAs).In many Mediterranean countries the management of beaches with banquettes is entrusted to regional and local governments.Often,beaches are an integral part of the tourist appeal and previous assessments on stakeholders’ views and management practices, make clear the need to promote better management of this littoral landscape on beaches.Enhanced support for more naturally-oriented practices from tourist sector or local authorities could be further increased with more capacity and knowledge of management options and policies for PAs.This project is based on the findings of the project Posbemed,developed within the past Interreg Med call with the aim of defining a joint strategy for the sustainable management of the beaches with banquettes.The objective of Posbemed 2 is to catalyse its results “to manage the Mediterranean coastline by developing planning strategies that recognize the value of the Posidonia beach-dune environment and integrate them into the overall coastal strategy,while also addressing concerns and educating stakeholders”.It further addresses key knowledge gaps,providing information that will enhance management decisions on adaptation,policies,planning and promotion in PAs.
en.expected_results: 1 Reference framework for improved coastline management tools for Protected Areas with Posidonia and its banquettes, is tested and refined at 11 sites. : A methodological framework and a recommendations tool-box for assessing and developing innovative coastal management approaches and good practices that will support the learning process. Through this, the project will enhance PA effectiveness and its resilience to climate change impacts. It will also provide optional measures to rehabilitate their environment, and contribute towards establishing and strengthening management at priority coastal and marine protected areas where Posidonia occurs. This will enhance the conservation of their natural resources and ecological processes and functionality while maintaining a sustainable use of resources.1 Reference framework for improved coastline management tools for Protected Areas with Posidonia and its banquettes, is tested and refined at 11 sites. : A methodological framework and a recommendations tool-box for assessing and developing innovative coastal management approaches and good practices that will support the learning process. Through this, the project will enhance PA effectiveness and its resilience to climate change impacts. It will also provide optional measures to rehabilitate their environment, and contribute towards establishing and strengthening management at priority coastal and marine protected areas where Posidonia occurs. This will enhance the conservation of their natural resources and ecological processes and functionality while maintaining a sustainable use of resources.2 Local and regional capacity is strengthened for effective best management practices it the Posidonia littoral zone : Increase knowledge and skills among stakeholders (administrations of both regional and local authorities as well as of stakeholders from civil society and private sector) to monitor, evaluate and report on the effectiveness and risks of local actions, planning and actual management of seagrass meadows, beaches and adjacent coastal dunes of protected areas.3 Gradual improvement in the effectiveness of instruments for Posidonia system and its banquettes for integrated spatial planning & coastline manage : The project will enable the development of aframework for transferring, disseminating and capitalising best practices for decision making and policy on Posidonia banquette conservation management, together with better integration of the Posidonia coastal landscape in protected sites and other coastal areas in policy planning and implementation. Seminars, workshops and key publications will be carried out to enhance networking of regional and local administrations, research centers and interested stakeholder groups to discuss and to capitalize best practices with the promotion of policy recommendations and guidelines for the private sector. A long-term contribution to a sustainable local business environment through better protection of the coastline from erosion.The gradual implementation of better policies at the local scale in the PAs and surrounding areas will help to achieve a more resilient Mediterranean protected area network.
en.achievements: Covid-19 and the subsequent restrictions implemented at national and international levels caused some activities to be postponed or rearranged in format. For different WPs and WP3 in particular, the restrictions caused a delay in the implementation of activities and therefore extended the timeline needed for developing a framework action plan. However, although slowed down, the activities never stopped, contributing to the gradual achievement of the objectives, even if to a minor degree. No output was expected to be completed in these two semesters.PPs started to:-	contact decision makers, MPAs and relevant stakeholders in order to discuss about project content and objectives and share strategies for future actions to be implemented and that best maintain good environmental conservation status in protected areas and reduce the impacts of management-	increase awareness of stakeholders to enhance their understanding of the value of Posidonia coastal/marine landscapes mainly through social media, POSBEMED2 web site and press-	share experiences with other EU projects and programs, different actors, decision makers, relevant stakeholders and the private sector as well in order to provide better integration of different points of view in the future planning tools to be incorporated into management planning and coastal policies where the Posidonia littoral zone occurs, across the Mediterranean.In this way, Posbemed 2 has already started to enable different actors to address a common challenge, to promote better management of the Posidonia littoral landscape by enhancing the value of natural beaches for sustainable tourism and local development, while ensuring high level conservation of priority sites (PAs).
en.expected_outputs: Action framework plan for Posidonia coastal zone at 8 PAs :8, Integrated and transferable methodology and toolbox for Posidonia beach management in PAs :1, Transferability of knowledge and stakeholder engagement on good practices for PA´s effectiveness enhanced :5, Set of policy recommendations and tools for integrated management of Posidonia littoral zone in PAs :4, Joint Charter of Commitment for Mediterranean Municipalities :1.
en.delivered_outputs: Number of joint governance plans, Surface of habitats supported to attain a better conservation status, Number of protected areas engaged (through charters, protocols, MoU) in implementing management strategies, Expenditures declared to the EC for Axis 3,

fr.description: Posbemed2 réunit les acteurs impliqués dans la gestion des côtes méditerranéennes,afin de tester des actions visant à une gestion durable du paysage et des plages avec des banquettes de Posidonie dans des aires protégées(AP).Dans les pays méditerranéens,la gestion des plages avec banquettes est confiée souvent aux gouvernements régionaux et locaux.De précédentes études montrent la nécessité de promouvoir une meilleure gestion des paysages littoraux abritant des banquettes.Le développement de pratiques de gestion plus naturelles des plages avec banquettes pourrait être renforcé,en améliorant les compétences et les connaissances du gestion et de politiques liés aux AP des acteurs du secteur touristique ou des autorités locales.Posbemed2 est basé sur les conclusions du projet Posbemed qui a défini une stratégie commune pour la gestion durable des plages avec des banquettes.L’objectif est de catalyser ces résultats,»pour gérer le littoral méditerranéen en développant des stratégies de planification qui reconnaissent la valeur environnementale des plages-dunes de Posidonie,et qui les intègrent dans une stratégie côtière plus globale tout en répondant aux préoccupations des parties prenantes et en leurs transmettant les bonnes pratiques de gestion».Ce second volet aborde aussi la question des principales lacunes de connaissances,pour améliorer les décisions en matière d'adaptation,de politiques,de planification pour la gestion et la promotion des banquettes dans les AP.
fr.expected_outputs: Plan-cadre d'action pour la zone côtière de Posidonia dans 8 AP :8, Méthodologie intégrée et transférable et boîte à outils pour la gestion des plages de Posidonia dans les AP :1,Transférabilité des connaissances et engagement des parties prenantes sur les bonnes pratiques pour améliorer l'efficacité de l'AP :5, Ensemble de recommandations politiques et d'outils pour la gestion intégrée de la zone littorale de Posidonia dans les AP :4, Charte commune d'engagement pour les municipalités méditerranéennes :1.
fr.delivered_outputs: Nombre de plans de gouvernance communs, Surface d'habitats soutenus pour atteindre un meilleur statut de conservation, Nombre de secteurs protégés engagés (par des chartes, des protocoles, le MoU) dans la mise en œuvre de stratégies de gestion, Dépenses déclarées à la CE pour Axe 3,

en.name: Empowering innovation capacity of SMEs, maritime clusters and networks in MED islands and coastal areas to support blue circular economy growth in fishing/aquaculture
en.description: BLUEfasma integrates and implements Circular Economy(CE) principles in the key blue growth sector of fishing/aquaculture to benefit MED insular/coastal areas in a novel/innovative way. Project team tackles the transnational challenge of continual depletion of natural resources and the below-EU-average MED CE innovation performance in fishing/aquaculture. Its overall objective is to empower innovation capacity of SMEs, maritime clusters/networks, PAs to boost blue CE growth in insular/coastal areas. CE prevents depletion of resources by closing energy & materials loops, leading to smart/sustainable growth as a key MED joint asset. Currently PAs/SMEs are cautious to embrace CE practices, which PAs perceive as cumbersome & SMEs as non-profitable. BLUEfasma approach will change current thinking on circularity by:-replicating effective CE work (eg in efficient use of waste & by-products), mainly from North-EU&MED projects-testing the upgraded circularity self-assessment tool in 11 participating MED territories and monitoring business readiness/willingness to invest in R&I -organising Blue Living Labs & targeted transferring activities -shifting policy towards CE-systematizing funding opportunities for blue CE innovation business investmentMain outputs: -BLUEfasma online platform-Blue Living Labs-BLUEfasma Capacity Building Instrument-Capitalization eventsThe outputs will increase innovation capacity and transnational activities among blue actors of Inter. MED Community.
en.expected_results: 1 Systematized knowledge and increased innovation capacities of SMEs and maritime clusters and networks : This project result focuses on the enhancement of innovation capacities of SMEs and maritime clusters & networks in blue CE in fishing/aquaculture.Project result’s concrete changes:a) a consolidated knowledge base that will systematise the already identified (during project preparation) best practices/tools/solutions/methods from past/ongoing projects (e.g. MISTRAL, CIRCULAROCEAN, HortiblueC, GRASPINNO)b) a CE readiness index that will be used as a unified MED measure of SMEs readiness and willingness to invest in CE and act as a decision support tool; based on the upgraded self-assessment tool on business circularity, initially developed in CIRTOINNO project. The index will be designed to have high transferability to any coastal/insular area. a and b will be hosted in BLUEfasma online platform, whose viability will be ensured by PPs, and will be ready for uptake by maritime clusters and networks; uptake will be facilitated by the systematic and co-creative approach of Blue Living Labs.Significant contribution to programme result indicator:Clusters/networks will increase their innovation capacity by offering their members a consolidated mix of services related to blue CE. They will support SMEs to use BLUEfasma services, improve their knowledge, have access to transferable tools/methods, and be trained (by PPs) in using them. SMEs will identify their readiness/willingness to invest in R&I in blue CE based on results from testing the CE readiness index.1 Systematized knowledge and increased innovation capacities of SMEs and maritime clusters and networks : This project result focuses on the enhancement of innovation capacities of SMEs and maritime clusters & networks in blue CE in fishing/aquaculture.Project result’s concrete changes:a) a consolidated knowledge base that will systematise the already identified (during project preparation) best practices/tools/solutions/methods from past/ongoing projects (e.g. MISTRAL, CIRCULAROCEAN, HortiblueC, GRASPINNO)b) a CE readiness index that will be used as a unified MED measure of SMEs readiness and willingness to invest in CE and act as a decision support tool; based on the upgraded self-assessment tool on business circularity, initially developed in CIRTOINNO project. The index will be designed to have high transferability to any coastal/insular area. a and b will be hosted in BLUEfasma online platform, whose viability will be ensured by PPs, and will be ready for uptake by maritime clusters and networks; uptake will be facilitated by the systematic and co-creative approach of Blue Living Labs.Significant contribution to programme result indicator:Clusters/networks will increase their innovation capacity by offering their members a consolidated mix of services related to blue CE. They will support SMEs to use BLUEfasma services, improve their knowledge, have access to transferable tools/methods, and be trained (by PPs) in using them. SMEs will identify their readiness/willingness to invest in R&I in blue CE based on results from testing the CE readiness index.2 Reinforced and increased cooperation and networking facilities with transnational scope : This project result focuses on reinforcing the transnational cooperation and networking between SMEs, clusters/networks, mentors/business angels, PAs and research centres. Project result’s concrete changes:a) e-network establishment to facilitate experience exchange & networking b) Blue Livings Labs (BLLs) to integrate R&I and create user-centered open innovation ecosystems focused on circularity increase in fishing/aquaculture c) effective synergies between businesses, research bodies & PAs, launched in BLLs/transferring eventsd) signed MoUs/Agreement between key stakeholders to ensure project results durabilityThe contribution to result indicator is significant as the “International Cooperation & Networking” is 1 of 5 key types of services of innovative clusters/networks (TACTICS studies, EU Cluster Observatory & EU Cluster Portal). Clusters/networks will increase their transnational effectiveness, leveraging collaborations arisen through above 4 actions. Benchmarking of best practices, deployment of facilities for advocacy, lobbying and liaison offices will support blue growth clusters/networks to bring their CE innovations to market. BLLs will offer hands-on training to involved peers of the ecosystem, user-centered open experiments and cross-fertilization of knowledge in blue CE aspects. Local enterprises will foster their competitiveness by B2B meetings & mentoring/coaching schemes during BLLs/transferring events organized by PPs.3 Increased access to financing opportunities/tools, tailored mentoring schemes and policy recommendations/shifting towards CE : This project result focuses on financing opportunities/tools, tailored mentoring schemes and policy recommendations/shifting related to blue CE. Project result’s concrete changes:a) collected financing opportunities for business investment in R&I in blue CEb) approaches for engaging PAs to provide their own funds to support blue CE in their regionc) tailored coaching/mentoring services/schemes to clusters; focused on providing access to public funding opportunities and on improving SMEs fund-raising capacity through Business Angels Networks, crowd-funding facility and brokerage/matchmaking eventsd) systematized policy inputs by Thematic Working Groupse) recommendations to policy makers and funding operators to allocate funding for R&I in SMEs/clusters/networks f) white paper proposing solutions to overcome barriers in blue CEg) policy shifting towards CE in fishing/aquacultureDirect contribution to programme result indicator:The above actions will support SMEs entrance in blue CE in fishing/aquaculture. Mentors/business angels with CE expertise, and experts like EASME and EAFIP, will support clusters/networks mainly in accessing innovative public/private funding opportunities. PPs will facilitate clusters/networks’ accessibility to financing tools such as Horizon2020, EIB, JESSICA. (b)&(e) will have a boosting effect in blue CE market and this will increase the market share of respective cluster/network members.
en.achievements:
BLUEfasma’s progress in the 5th reporting period was satisfactory to the greatest extent, since the activities/deliverables were carried out according to the set time plan. Also the extended deadline for some activities/deliverables (as voted by the Steering Committee) was respected. The close cooperation between partners (PPs) was effective and led to the successful achievement of all project objectives. For the proper project coordination and internal communication between PPs meetings took place, and tracking and internal evaluation reports were performed periodically. In addition, external communication and capitalizing activities were carried out. Concerning WP1, LP & PPs implemented the necessary administrative/financial activities ensuring the on-time 4th and 5th Payment Claims submission. The external expert hired by LP submitted the Project Assessment Report. In WP2, PPs updated the Communication Plan; published the 5th e-newsletter; developed additional promotional material; organized open day events and participated in 4 events organised by Interreg MED HPs & 5 external events. PPs updated BLUEfasma website & social media regularly. In WP5, PPs updated the Capitalisation (CAP) Plan and organized 9 National & 2 Transnational CAP events in Barcelona and Budva, plus 1 CAP event for South Mediterranean countries in Cairo bridging the two shores of the Mediterranean towards Circular Economy and expanding project’s area of intervention in these countries; successfully achieving project’s output 

5.2 and promoting project achievements to a higher political level. 4 Thematic Working Group meetings took place focusing on the mainstreaming of BLUEfasma results and ensuring the effective implementation of WP5 deliverables. PPs worked on the development of the roadmap for wider uptake of BLUEfasma online platform, the policy recommendations, and transferring CE readiness index in other sectors; developed the capacity building instrument which is available on the BLUEfasma online platform contributing in project’s output 

5.1 achievement; upgraded the circularity self-assessment tool in local languages & Arabic. The development of the Letter of Commitment signed by 26 entities committing in policy shift towards Circular Economy in fishing/aquaculture, and the Agreement signed by 43 entities committing for following up project results after the end of the BLUEfasma project are highlighted. PPs participated in lobbying events and had close cooperation with HPs.
en.expected_outputs: BLUEfasma online platform :1, Findings from Blue Living Labs :11, BLUEfasma Capacity Building Instrument :5, Capitalisation events :1.
en.delivered_outputs: Number of operational instruments to favour innovation of SMEs, Number of enterprises receiving grants, Number of enterprises receiving non-financial support, Number of transnational innovation clusters supported, Expenditures declared to the EC for Axis 1,

fr.name: Renforcer la capacité d'innovation des PME, des clusters et des réseaux maritimes dans les îles MED et les zones côtières de soutenir la croissance de l'économie bleue circulaire dans la pêche/l'aquaculture
fr.description: BLUEfasma implémente de manière novatrice les principes de l’Economie Circulaire(EC) dans le secteur clé de croissance bleue -pêche/aquaculture- au bénéfice des régions MED insulaires/maritimes.L’équipe-projet aborde le défi transnational de l’épuisement des ressources & déficit d’innovation en EC de peche/aquaculture en MED comparé aux standards européens.Son but est d’accroitre la capacité d’innovation des PME & PA, des réseaux et communautés maritimes/insulaires, afin de promouvoir l’EC dans ces régions.EC prévient l’épuisement des ressources en utilisant matériaux & énergie en boucles fermées:capitalisation des pratiques en MED mènerait a une croissance intelligente/durable.PA et PME ont aujourd’hui des préjugés freinant leurs pratiques d’EC –PA imaginent une lourdeur d’implémentation & PME une non-profitabilité. BLUEfasma déconstruira ces préjugés en:-reproduisant des approches efficaces d’EC pré-existantes (projets N.EU/MED)-testant outil avancé d’auto-évaluation en EC sur 11 MED pilotes & surveillant capacité/volonté des PME a investir en R&I -organisant Laboratoires Bleus Vivants & transférant leurs activités-remodellant politiques d’EC-systématisant opportunités de financement des investissements pour l’innovation en EC bleue Productions principales:e-plateforme BLUEfasma;Lab Bleus Vivants;Outil d’évaluation d’EC bleu BLUEfasma;Actions de mutualisation.Ces productions accroitront capacité d’innovation & dynamiques transnationales au sein de communauté MED.
fr.delivered_outputs: Nombre d’instruments opérationnels en faveur de l’innovation des PME, Nombre d'entreprises béneficiant de subventions, Nombre d'entreprises recevant un soutien non-financier, Nombre de clusters d'innovation transnationaux soutenus, Dépenses déclarées à la CE pour Axe 1,

en.name: BEYOND EUROPEAN SUSTAINABLE TOURISM MED PATH
en.description: BEST MED contributes to the objective of enhancing MED Governance, being the main challenges seasonality and lack of effective cooperation among main tourism actors, including the citizen active participation on the policies designPartnership of 10 regions and 11 partners, commitmment of 11 associated partners is involved in this Project that contributes to support evidence-based planning, monitoring and evaluation of interventions in MED programme, by getting a joint approach on “green” planning based on and improving the outputs gained by other projects such as Tourism Data indicators toolkit and Green Beach Model (MITOMED+), and Tourism sustainability toolkit in Mediterranean (CO-EVOLVE) on a widen model for routes linking coastal and maritime and inland destinations, as well as other projects outputsIt aims to have a new integrated and sustainable touristic planning aiming at the mitigation of seasonality in the MED area, through the connection between coastal regions to inland regions, such as a path-route method. A testing phase will obtain a joint model that will be transfered and capitalised as well as the toolkit and DATA indicators updatedWe propose to analyse positive and negative impacts of tourism on the quality of life of tourists, residents and about the quality of the tourist's experience, proposed in the Faro Convention AP 2019 COEA MoU and Granada Charter on Sustainable Tourism will be signed for transferability and sustainability of outputs
en.expected_results: 1 Data knowledge improvement: Comparable and usable data collection, by also making data accessible : -1 Data knowledge improvement: Comparable and usable data collection, by also making data accessible : -2 Enhanced participatory and integrated multilevel planning: Active involvement of MED territories through cross-sectorial linkages contributing to info : -3 Improved policy environment and processes in tourism: Simplification of the policy framework to improve the business ecosystem. : -4 More sustainable tourism: Demonstrated contribution to the development of more sustainable transnational tourism offer in the MED area and its diversi : -
en.achievements:
During the first period, the partnership has worked to stablish different mechanisms of coordination for an adequate management and internal/external communication. For this purpose, Best Med Project attended to the LP Seminar organised by the JS/Interreg MED in Marseille last 10th and 11th December, 2019 where the project was presented. In this framework, the 1st SCM and KoM were organised in Malta last 30th and 31st January, 

2020. During the SCM, the project's guidelines were presented and several mechanisms were discussed for coordination and to carry out the deliverables of the WPs. The management of WP1, WP2 and WP3 were discussed. Also, our Project Officer, Ms. Francesca Marcato attended and made a presentation about the Interreg MED Program and its architecture and the certification process. The Project Communication Officer of Interreg MED, Mr. Paolo Emerenciano, also attended and presented the framework for the development of WP2 Communication. On the other hand, Best Med made the project's presentation and gave several contributions as well in the Coordination Meeting organised by Interreg Med and Panoramed in Madrid last 19th and 20th February, 

2020. The participation in these meetings was crucial for Best Med in order to stablish the basis for the collaboration of the project with Horizontal Projects (HPs), other Strategic Projects (SPs), specially Smartmed, and PANORAMED. This was the starting point of the mainstreaming activities that Best Med is currently supporting and implementing. These mainstream actions leaded by Interreg MED are focused on the kowledge and good practices in the MED area in relation to sustainable tourism. Furhtermore, in terms of coordination with the MED Sustainable Community, Best Med began to work in this period by integrating itself in this community building and searching for synergies and identifying the most important elements of the project (outputs, needs and expectations).Our Horizontal Angel from Med Tourism Community is Barcelona Council whom we have opened an interesting line of collaboration. For last, and in reference to internal and external communication, Best Med made a project's group in CIRCABC platform (Communication and Information Resource Centre for Administration, Businesses and Citizens) of the European Commision, which is a very valuable tool to share documents and been connected. Lastly, Best Med opened 1 Twitter and 1 Linkedin profile and started the works for the project's web.
en.expected_outputs: Number of structures involved in key activities :22, Number of joint governance plans proposed and admitted :20, Number of public organizations at regional and sub-regional engaged through Agreements in implementing sustainable plan and the future application of the Key Performance Indicators :20, Number of stakeholders involved and participating in the focus groups :10, Number of urban areas engaged through Joint Seminars :10, Number of events organized for knowledge transferringq :48, Number of transnational clusters :120, Number of regions and sub-regions engaged in sustainability :20.
en.delivered_outputs: Number of stakeholders (structures) involved in supported initiatives (per category representing public and private stakeholders), Expenditures declared to the EC for Axis 4,

fr.delivered_outputs: Nombre de stakeholders (structures) impliquées dans initiatives soutenues (par représentation de catégorie d'organisations publiques et privées), Dépenses déclarées à la CE pour Axe 4,
